topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at reforming student loan policies and repayment options in the United States.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to set the interest rate on Direct Loans
Requires any savings to the federal government that result from this Act's amendment to be used for deficit reduction.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to revise federal student loan repayment plan options
Establishes a new revised pay-as-you-earn repayment plan that caps monthly payments at 10% of discretionary income.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from the gross income of an employee up to $5000 per year of the amounts paid by an employer under a student loan payment assistance program
Defines 'student loan payment assistance' as the payment of principal or interest on any indebtedness incurred by an employee solely to pay qualified higher education expenses.

topic.name: Voting Rights Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to voting and ensuring the integrity of the electoral process in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require each state to permit any eligible individual on the day of a federal election to register to vote, and cast a vote.
Election Day Registration Act
Document 2: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 with respect to voter verification and mandatory auditing by state officials.
Count Every Vote Act of 2005
Document 3: Amends the Uniformed and Overseas Citizens Absentee Voting Act to require the presidential designee to have experience in election administration.
Fixing the Federal Voting Assistance Program Act of 2009

topic.name: Renewable Energy and Emissions Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and regulating emissions from power generation facilities.

Document 1: construct, operate, and maintain on a reimbursable basis battery recharging stations
requires the AOC to charge Senators and covered employees fees for the electricity
Document 2: amends the Clean Air Act to provide for a standard of performance for new electric generating facilities
establish a carbon dioxide allowance trading program
Document 3: requires states to update their building energy efficiency codes
establish a National Bioenergy Partnership to coordinate efforts between governmental entities and the private sector

topic.name: Medicare Policy Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to Medicare aimed at improving care, preventing fraud, and adjusting payment mechanisms.

Document 1: Amends part C of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to evaluate the MA and the health status risk adjustment payment mechanisms
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to conduct an additional site inspection
Requires that the information in the health integrity protection database (HIPDB) to be available to certain health care providers
Document 3: Declares that between October 1, 2008, and March 31, 2009, specified regulations regarding the formula for determining the federal rate for inpatient hospital capital-related costs
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to delay for six months following enactment of the Medicaid outpatient hospital services regulation proposed on September 28, 2007

topic.name: Immigration Enforcement Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the enforcement of immigration laws and the reporting of related statistics.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to subject all illegal alien entrants to fines.
Directs United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement to provide for: (1) detention of illegal aliens.
Document 2: Prohibits the payment of funds to a state unless such state compiles statistics on persons arrested, charged, convicted of a crime, or incarcerated by such state, including the immigration status.
Requires the FBI to annually publish all statistics reported under this Act.

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts related to sanctions imposed by the United States on countries such as Iran and Cuba, focusing on trade restrictions and national security measures.

Document 1: Iran Ballistic Missile Sanctions Act of 2016
require sanctions on Iran and related entities and persons for actions to acquire or develop ballistic missiles
Document 2: United States-Cuba Normalization Act of 2015
repeal the embargo on trade with Cuba
Document 3: Sanction Iran, Safeguard America Act of 2015
eliminate authority to waive sanctions relating to transportation of crude oil from Iran

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension or reduction of duties on specific chemical compounds and mixtures.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
temporary suspension of duty
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on Acetic acid, [4-2,6-dihydro-2,6-dioxo-7-phenylbenzo[1,2-b:4,5-b']difuan-3yl)phenoxy]-, 2-ethoxyethyl ester.
extend the temporary suspension of duty
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to reduce temporarily the duty on mixtures of imidacloprid and cyfluthrin.
reduce temporarily the duty on mixtures

topic.name: Aviation Security Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing security and safety measures in the aviation industry, particularly regarding cybersecurity and baggage handling.

Document 1: require domestic or foreign air carriers and manufacturers of aircraft to disclose to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) any attempted or successful cyberattack
improve the regulations to incorporate requirements relating to cybersecurity into the requirements for obtaining an air carrier operating certificate
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to issue regulations to limit the size of baggage and personal items carried on board a passenger aircraft
requires the TSA and FAA to notify passengers via their websites of federal carry-on baggage and personal item limitations
Document 3: requires maintenance work on passenger aircraft to be performed by certain authorized individuals
Prohibits the Administrator from certifying a foreign repair station unless it complies with certain final security regulations

topic.name: U.S. Food and Economic Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to food aid, agricultural policy, and economic development aimed at enhancing food security and economic competitiveness in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes the Food for Peace program in the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) under the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961.
Authorizes USAID to provide emergency and nonemergency foreign assistance, including through the provision of U.S. commodities or local or regional procurement.
Document 2: Amends the Public Works and Economic Development Act of 1965 to authorize appropriations for economic development assistance programs under such Act for FY2011-FY2015.

Includes among the purposes for which assistance may be provided encouraging and supporting public-private partnerships that promote innovation and entrepreneurship that is critical to economic competitiveness.
Document 3: Repeals direct payments under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to notify farm owners regarding their opportunity to elect to retain base acres.

topic.name: Tax and Financial Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at modifying tax codes and financial regulations to influence economic factors such as research funding, payroll taxes, and insurance rates.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the alternative simplified tax credit for research expenses
increase the alternative simplified tax credit for research expenses to 20%
Document 2: Amends the Tax Relief, Unemployment Insurance Reauthorization, and Job Creation Act of 2010
extend through 2012 the 2% reduction in employment tax rates
Document 3: bars from taking effect for a 12-month period any increases in mandatory chargeable risk premium rates
increases in chargeable risk premium rates shall be made on a yearly basis

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the suspension and modification of duties on specific goods.

Document 1: extend the temporary suspension of duty on staple fibers of viscose rayon
measuring 1.67 to 16.67 decitex and having a fiber length each measuring 20 mm or more but not over 150 mm.
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Sulfur Black 1.
Document 3: modify the article description of certain women's footwear
extend the temporary reduction of duty on such footwear.

topic.name: Gulf Coast Environmental Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to environmental safety and management in the Gulf Coast region, particularly focusing on pipeline safety, fishing regulations, and emergency response to environmental disasters.

Document 1: Revises certain minimum pipeline safety standards requirements
requirement that the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) issue a regulation
Document 2: Waives requirements for coastwise endorsements to a certificate of documentation
foreign vessels engaged in containment, remediation, or associated activities
Document 3: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act
exclusive fishery management authority over reef fish

topic.name: Employment and Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to employment benefits, including health coverage reporting and unemployment compensation adjustments.

Document 1: requires the Department of Treasury to implement and maintain a voluntary prospective reporting system for employers
permits electronic delivery of employee statement if employee consented previously
Document 2: declares that for any week in whole or in part within a month that an individual is paid or determined to be eligible for unemployment compensation he or she shall be deemed to have engaged in substantial gainful activity
disqualified from receiving Social Security disability benefits after a certain period has elapsed
Document 3: revises the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account
extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation

topic.name: Tax Credits and Reforms
topic.description: Legislation related to tax credits and reforms affecting education and health benefits.

Document 1: tax credit for 50% of qualified tuition and related expenses
attendance at an institution of higher education
Document 2: repeal of the excise tax on employer-sponsored health care coverage
high-cost plans
Document 3: exclude from gross income payments received by a student
comprehensive student work-learning-service program

topic.name: Native American Legislation
topic.description: Legislation addressing the rights, health, and land management of Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Native American Suicide Prevention Act of 2015
consult with each federally recognized Indian tribe
Document 2: takes specified federal land within the Park into trust for the Quileute Indian Tribe
requires the Secretary of the Interior to take specified nonfederal land owned by the Tribe into trust
Document 3: Minnesota Chippewa Tribe Judgment Fund Distribution Act of 2011
requires the Tribe to provide the Secretary with updated membership rolls

topic.name: Transportation and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to funding and safety improvements in transportation infrastructure.

Document 1: Northeast Corridor Rail Tunnel Safety Act
authorize appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation for Amtrak use
Document 2: Funding for Aviation Screeners and Threat Elimination Restoration Act
repeals requirement that aviation security service fees be credited as offsetting receipts
Document 3: Amends the Legislative Branch Appropriations Act
donate computers and related peripheral tools to public schools

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing consumer rights and protections in financial and bankruptcy contexts.

Document 1: Amends federal bankruptcy law with respect to the requirement for a municipality to be a debtor under Chapter 9
Requires the municipality also to establish by clear and convincing evidence that it satisfies all eligibility criteria.
Document 2: This bill amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act to authorize a person or the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to furnish to a consumer reporting agency information relating to the performance of a consumer in making payments

Information about a consumer's usage of any utility or telecommunications services may be furnished to a consumer reporting agency only to the extent that such information relates to payment by the consumer for such services.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating campaign financing and lobbying activities in political elections.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide graduated income tax rates applicable to principal campaign committees.
Applies to all comparable committees of candidates for state and local offices.
Document 2: Amends the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995 to require quarterly contribution reports.
Includes names of federal candidates or officeholders for whom contributions are collected.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit the conversion of leadership PAC funds to personal use.

topic.name: Waterway and Energy Infrastructure
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and actions related to the management and development of waterway navigation systems and hydropower projects.

Document 1: Redesignates Lock and Dam No. 5 of the McClellan-Kerr Arkansas River Navigation System
authorized by the Rivers and Harbors Act
Document 2: authorizes the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to extend by six years
W. Kerr Scott Hydropower Project located on the Yadkin River

topic.name: Intelligence Authorization and Security
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the funding and oversight of U.S. intelligence activities and information security measures.

Document 1: authorizes FY2017 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
the amounts authorized, and the authorized personnel ceilings, are those in the classified Schedule of Authorizations
Document 2: authorizes FY2016 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
allows the DNI to authorize employment of civilian personnel in excess of the number authorized for FY2016
Document 3: requires federal agencies to perform an independent audit of their information security programs
establishes a Chief Information Security Officers Council to assist such Officers in developing best practices

topic.name: Immigration Reform for STEM and Entrepreneurs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at reforming immigration policies to attract skilled workers and entrepreneurs in STEM fields to the United States.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjust to conditional permanent resident status up to 50,000 aliens who have earned a master's or doctorate degree in a science, technology, engineering, or mathematics field
authorizes the Secretary to issue conditional immigrant visas to up to 75,000 qualified alien entrepreneurs.
Document 2: establish a pilot fee-based premium processing service to expedite visa interview appointments
authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate any country as a program country.
Document 3: make up to 50,000 visas available to qualified immigrants who possess a graduate degree at the level of master's or higher in a field of science, technology, engineering, or mathematics
eliminates the foreign residency requirement for certain foreign students.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Oversight
topic.description: The documents discuss the oversight and review processes of federal regulations and fiscal estimates to ensure informed legislative decision-making.

Document 1: ensure that Congress is presented with reliable information from the Congressional Budget Office (CBO)
prepare fiscal estimates of each proposed change in federal revenue law
Document 2: require any appointment to the Public Interest Declassification Board to be for three years
extend Board authority through 2014
Document 3: requires federal agencies to review their covered rules
provides for sunset review of a rule upon the request of a congressional committee

topic.name: Tax Policy in Energy Sector
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments to the Internal Revenue Code that impact tax regulations related to manufacturing facilities and the oil and gas industry.

Document 1: qualify manufacturing facilities that create intangible property for tax-exempt bond financing
revise the definition of 'manufacturing facility'
Document 2: exempt sales of natural gas for use in motor vehicles from the $5 million retail sales limitation
oil and gas depreciation allowance
Document 3: limit the oil depletion allowance
terminate accelerated depreciation of natural gas distribution lines

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and funding for small businesses through federal programs and initiatives.

Document 1: increases the government-wide goal for awards to small businesses
goal for award to small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans
Document 2: establishes a small business intermediary lending pilot program
make direct loans to eligible nonprofit lending intermediaries
Document 3: make grants to municipalities to promote community greening initiatives
develop and plan such an initiative, which may include revitalizing municipal parks

topic.name: Data Breach Notification Laws
topic.description: Legislation governing the requirements for notifying individuals and authorities about data breaches involving personal information.

Document 1: Requires certain commercial entities to protect and secure nonpublic personal information.
Directs entities to notify individuals whose personal information may have been accessed.
Document 2: Requires notification of U.S. residents following the discovery of a security breach.
Allows a delay of notification for law enforcement and national security purposes.

topic.name: Government Regulation and Taxation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at reforming government regulatory processes and tax policies to support business growth and efficiency.

Document 1: Requires the head of any federal or independent regulatory agency... to complete a review that... analyzes the rule's expected impact on state, local, and tribal governments, as well as on the ability of new businesses to form and expand.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide a permanent full tax exclusion on gain from the sale or exchange of qualified small business stock.
Document 2: Requires each applicant for a grant from an executive agency to submit in the grant application... a list of each similar federal grant applied for in the prior year.
Imposes penalties on grant applicants who fail to provide such information.
Document 3: Establishes the Corporate Entitlement Reform Commission to examine and identify federal programs and tax laws that provide corporate entitlements.
Recommend $50 billion in federal savings on corporate entitlements for the period beginning October 1, 2007, and ending September 30, 2017.

topic.name: Veterans' Health Care and Rehabilitation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at improving health care services, rehabilitation, and support for veterans, particularly those with traumatic brain injuries and other medical needs.

Document 1: individualized rehabilitation and reintegration plans for veterans with traumatic brain injury (TBI)
improving such veterans' behavioral and mental health functioning
Document 2: optimize the provision of critical care services to Medicare beneficiaries
family-centered approach to the provision of inpatient critical care services
Document 3: increase the number of GME residency positions at VA medical facilities
establish at least three graduate behavioral medicine residency programs in underserved areas

topic.name: Advanced Transportation Technologies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative initiatives aimed at developing and implementing advanced materials and technologies to enhance vehicle efficiency, reduce emissions, and improve transportation infrastructure.

Document 1: Directs the Secretaries of Transportation (DOT), Energy (DOE), Defense (DOD), and Homeland Security (DHS), to carry out a program to improve the nation's transportation infrastructure
strive to produce new composite materials, including related manufacturing processes or applications
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy (DOE) to conduct a program of basic and applied research, development, demonstration, and commercial application activities
to substantially reduce or eliminate petroleum use by, and emissions from, the nation's passenger and commercial vehicles
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Energy to conduct a program of research, development, demonstration, and commercial application of technologies needed for the development of plug-in hybrid electric vehicles
establish a competitive grant pilot demonstration program to provide up to 25 grants annually to state and local governments

topic.name: Government Land Conveyance
topic.description: Documents discussing the transfer of federally owned land for local development purposes.

Document 1: Authorizes the Department of the Army to convey all right, title, and interest of the United States in certain real property
to improve arterial transportation routes in the vicinity of Fort Hood
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to Miami-Dade County, Florida, federally owned land
for the purpose of building a fire station.

topic.name: Evidence-Based Policy and Health
topic.description: This topic encompasses the development and implementation of policies based on rigorous data analysis and health assessments to improve public health and environmental standards.

Document 1: establishes in the executive branch a Commission on Evidence-Based Policymaking
make recommendations on how best to incorporate outcomes measurement, institutionalize randomized controlled trials, and rigorous impact analysis into program design.
Document 2: require the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to specify, in performance criteria for monitoring and assessing coastal recreation waters
develop and implement a coastal recreation waters pollution source identification and tracking program for such waters that are used by the public
Document 3: evaluate the health effects of housing-related health hazards for which limited research or understanding of causes or associations exists
expand current indoor environmental monitoring efforts to establish baseline levels of indoor chemical pollutants and their sources.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax deductions, allowances, and treatment of various financial assets and liabilities.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to remove certain limitations on the tax deduction for employee use of cellular telephones
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a five-year carryback for net operating losses incurred in 2007, 2008, or 2009
increase to $250,000 in 2008 the expensing allowance for certain depreciable business assets
Document 3: amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from gross income long-term gain on capital assets acquired between the date of enactment of this Act and December 31, 2009

topic.name: Coast Guard and Maritime Policy
topic.description: Legislation concerning the funding, operations, and administration of the Coast Guard and maritime transportation.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010-FY2011 for the Coast Guard
research, development, test, and evaluation programs related to maritime technology
Document 3: Authorizes FY2015 appropriations for the Coast Guard
research, development, test, and evaluation of technologies, materials, and human factors related to search and rescue

topic.name: Tax Incentives and Relief
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at providing tax deductions, credits, and relief for specific groups and activities, including businesses, conservation efforts, and adoption expenses.

Document 1: allows a standard tax deduction for business use of a residence
provides for an inflation adjustment to the standard deduction
Document 2: treats gain or loss from the sale of a mitigation bank credit as a capital asset
allows preferential tax treatment of such gain or loss
Document 3: expands the adoption tax credit and adoption assistance programs
allows the tax credit for adoption expenses in the taxable year in which such expenses are paid

topic.name: Health Insurance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to health insurance coverage and regulations affecting small businesses and specific health screenings.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the small employer health care insurance tax credit
increase the employee threshold from 10 to 20 employees for purposes of determining the maximum allowable amount of such credit
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act, the Employee Retirement Income Security Act (ERISA), and the Internal Revenue Code to require a group health plan to provide coverage for colorectal screening
Sets forth exceptions, including limiting the amount of coinsurance for such screenings and limiting waiting periods
Document 3: Sets forth provisions regulating lawsuits for health care liability claims concerning the provision of health care goods or services
Limits noneconomic damages to $250,000

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative acts related to land management, conservation, and the conveyance of public lands for specific purposes.

Document 1: directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the town...
requires the town to use the land conveyed under this Act only for public purposes.
Document 2: designates specified wilderness areas in New Mexico...
requires the development of separate management plans for such areas.
Document 3: allows the state of Utah to select lands under the administrative jurisdiction of the Bureau of Land Management...
in fulfillment of certain land grants made to Utah upon being admitted as a state.

topic.name: Coastal and Forest Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, restoration, and protection of coastal zones and forest resources in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coastal Zone Management Act of 1972 to authorize appropriations for grants under provisions relating to administration of a state's coastal zone management program.
Increases... the portion of amounts appropriated to carry out provisions relating to administration of a state's coastal zone management program and resource management improvement.
Document 2: Amends the Cooperative Forestry Assistance Act of 1978 to direct the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to establish a landscape-scale restoration program.
Enhance public benefits from trees and forests, as identified in a state-wide assessment and a long-term state-wide forest resource strategy under the Act.
Document 3: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to provide for expedited consultation and conferencing with respect to impacts on endangered or threatened species.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior or of Commerce... to relocate from the flood control levee... any threatened or endangered species of plant or animal.

topic.name: Federal Grant and Service Programs
topic.description: Legislation focused on the reauthorization and improvement of federal grant and service programs, including community service, transparency in grant awarding, and support for small business innovation.

Document 1: Amends the National and Community Service Act of 1990
increasing service opportunities for retiring professionals
Document 2: Establishes new standards to promote transparency in the awarding of federal grants
requires each executive agency to use and make publicly available merit-based selection procedures
Document 3: reauthorize and make permanent the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs
enhanced small business access to federal innovation investments

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments and Provisions
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and provisions related to the Internal Revenue Code, focusing on tax deductions, credits, and reforms aimed at businesses and individuals.

Document 1: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the additional 50% depreciation allowance, known as bonus depreciation, for depreciable business property.
The bill also makes permanent the election to increase the alternative minimum tax credit limitation in lieu of bonus depreciation.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2015 expiring tax provisions pertaining to individual and business taxpayers and the energy sector.
Expresses the sense of the Senate that a process of comprehensive tax reform should commence in the 114th Congress.

topic.name: Intelligence Oversight and Surveillance
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures related to foreign intelligence surveillance, security clearance investigations, and the establishment of oversight mechanisms to protect privacy rights.

Document 1: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to add a new title concerning additional procedures for acquiring the communications of certain persons outside the United States.
Requires all certifications to be submitted to the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court (Court) for review.
Document 2: Amends the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 to expand the responsibilities of the executive agency selected to conduct security clearance investigations.
Requires personnel security investigations of an individual to include information collected from criminal or civil legal proceedings.
Document 3: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to establish as an independent office in the executive branch the Office of the Privacy Advocate General.
Requires the Privacy Advocate General to serve as the opposing counsel with respect to any application by the federal government for an order or directive.

topic.name: Education and Development Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative initiatives aimed at enhancing educational outcomes through social, emotional, and literacy development in children.

Document 1: requires the National Center for Education Research to carry out research regarding the impact of social and emotional education
defines 'social and emotional learning' as the processes through which students acquire and effectively apply self-awareness and self-management skills
Document 2: creates a Pediatric Early Literacy program under which the Secretary of Education awards matching grants to nonprofit organizations
health care providers encourage parents to read aloud to their children and offer them recommendations and strategies for doing so
Document 3: requires the Director of the National Science Foundation (NSF) to establish a nanotechnology in the schools program
provide nanotechnology education to students and teachers

topic.name: Inland Waterway Infrastructure and Flood Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the construction, rehabilitation, and management of inland waterways and flood protection systems in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Army...for navigation infrastructure of inland and intracoastal waterways.
20-year program for making capital investments on inland and intracoastal waterways.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Army to carry out the hurricane and storm damage reduction project...
federal responsibility for inland waterway transportation.
Document 3: Modifies the project for flood damage reduction...to authorize the Chief of Engineers to construct improvements.
Requires the nonfederal interest to receive credit for expenses and in-kind contributions.

topic.name: Veterans Benefits Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving and adjusting benefits for veterans and their families, including cost-of-living adjustments and eligibility criteria for various compensation programs.

Document 1: CPI-E Act of 2014 - Bases cost-of-living increases in benefits
Consumer Price Index for the elderly
Document 2: Amends the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009
proof of service in determining eligibility for Filipino Veterans Equity Compensation Fund
Document 3: Veterans Burial Benefits Improvement Act of 2007
Increases the authorized allowance for burial and funeral expenses for deceased veterans

topic.name: Financial Literacy and Investment Protection
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing financial literacy and protecting investors in the context of personal savings, bankruptcy, and fraudulent investment schemes.

Document 1: Establishes an ASPIRE Fund in the Treasury, including a Lifetime Savings Account.
Instructs the Secretary of the Treasury, in coordination with the Financial Literacy and Education Commission, to develop programs to promote the financial literacy of account holders.
Document 2: Amends federal bankruptcy law to increase the trustee fee from $15 to $75.
Prohibits the waiver of the trustee fee if an individual debtor is able to pay in installments.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a special tax deduction for qualified fraudulent investment losses held in an individual retirement account (IRA).
Defines 'qualified fraudulent investment loss' as a loss discovered in 2008 or 2009 resulting from a fraudulent arrangement.

topic.name: Postal Facility Designations
topic.description: The documents discuss the designation of various United States Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals or events.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 2000 Mulford Road in Mulberry, Florida, as the 'Sergeant First Class Daniel M. Ferguson Post Office.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 229 West Main Cross Street in Findlay, Ohio, as the 'Michael Garver Oxley Memorial Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Designates the federal building located at 2030 Southwest 145th Avenue in Miramar, Florida, as the 'Benjamin P. Grogan and Jerry L. Dove Federal Building.'

topic.name: Trade and Employment Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at adjusting trade policies and providing assistance to workers affected by trade changes.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of funds made available by this Act for a project for the construction, alteration, maintenance, or repair of a public building or public work unless all of the iron, steel, and manufactured goods used in such project are produced in the United States (Buy American).
Requires all laborers and mechanics employed by contractors and subcontractors on federally-assisted projects to be paid wages at the locally prevailing rates (Davis-Bacon Act).
Document 2: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 to extend duty-free treatment under the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) through July 31, 2013.
Amends the Trade and Globalization Adjustment Assistance Act of 2009 to repeal the December 31, 2011, termination date for trade adjustment assistance (TAA) programs.
Document 3: Amends the Trade Act of 1974 (the Act) to allow the filing of a petition with the Secretary of Labor for certification of eligibility to apply for trade adjustment assistance (TAA) by adversely affected workers in a service sector firm or a public agency.

Provides for the certification for TAA of: (1) adversely affected workers in a public agency or its subdivision; and (2) adversely affected secondary workers in a service sector firm or its subdivision.

topic.name: Regulation of Reproductive Health
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal regulations and acts related to reproductive health services, including abortion, emergency contraception, and ammunition sales for reproductive health contexts.

Document 1: requires the licensing of ammunition dealers
prohibits any person except a licensed importer, manufacturer, or dealer from selling ammunition
Document 2: prohibits any federal funds from being provided to a hospital unless the hospital meets certain conditions related to a woman who is a victim of sexual assault
provides the woman with accurate and unbiased information about emergency contraception
Document 3: requires abortion providers to display ultrasound images
subjects an abortion provider who knowingly or recklessly fails to comply with this Act to civil penalties

topic.name: Regulatory Legislative Measures
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts aimed at regulating public health, safety, and communication standards.

Document 3: Amends the Public Telecommunications Act of 1992 to require the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to maintain a policy that a single word or image may constitute indecent programming.

topic.name: Support for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing support and protection for vulnerable groups such as veterans, children, and the elderly.

Document 1: expands assistance for mentally ill offenders and veterans
collaboration among criminal justice, veterans, and mental health agencies
Document 2: authorize grants for hiring child protective services workers
Document 3: ensure training in elder abuse prevention
establish National Adult Protective Services Resources Center

topic.name: Tariff Suspensions on Goods
topic.description: The documents discuss temporary suspensions of duties on various goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain fitness equipment.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on artificial flowers of man-made fibers assembled as picks.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain single yarns of viscose rayon.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Public Health Workforce and Services
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing the public health workforce and improving access to mental health and substance use disorder services.

Document 1: establish the Public Health Workforce Scholarship Program
provide for the repayment of loans incurred by individuals in the pursuit of the relevant public health workforce educational degree
Document 2: organize a national awareness campaign regarding serious mental illness
HHS must award grants for programs and services, including telehealth services provided by psychiatrists or psychologists
Document 3: provide funds to states to enable them to increase the wages paid to targeted direct support professionals
audit a sample from among the states in order to assess the effectiveness of progress made in reducing or eliminating the wage gap

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Actions
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative measures and acts related to transportation, international relations, and census procedures in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Department of Transportation and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1986
repeal the prohibition against use of certain funds for any segment of the downtown Los Angeles to San Fernando Valley Metro Rail project
Document 2: Taiwan Travel Act
U.S. policy to permit officials at all levels of the U.S. government to travel to Taiwan
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Commerce to include in each questionnaire used for conducting the decennial census of population
a question to ascertain U.S. citizenship and immigration status

topic.name: Food Safety and Labeling Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring the safety and proper labeling of food products, particularly those involving genetic engineering and chemical treatments.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem misbranded any food that has been genetically engineered or contains one or more genetically engineered ingredients
Defines 'genetically engineered' as a material intended for human consumption that is an organism produced through the intentional use of genetic engineering.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to make changes to the definition of 'color additive' to include carbon monoxide
Authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish alternative labeling requirements, not earlier than five years after the effective date of this Act.

topic.name: Legislation and Governance
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts related to governance, statehood, and legal authority within the District of Columbia and its surrounding jurisdictions.

Document 1: Amends the Lacey Act Amendments of 1981
prohibitions against the sale or possession of plants
Document 2: Sets forth procedures for admission into the United States of the state of New Columbia
Requires the Mayor of the District of Columbia to submit to the eligible voters propositions for statehood
Document 3: the authority to grant clemency for offenses against the District of Columbia shall be exercised by such person or persons

topic.name: Education Funding and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing educational resources and support for teachers and schools, particularly in underserved areas and following disasters.

Document 1: extend through 2012 the tax deduction for expenses of elementary and secondary school teachers
expand the definition of 'school' for tax deduction to include a home school
Document 2: awarding grants to local educational agencies for areas affected by Hurricane Katrina
provide annual bonuses to teachers and school leaders
Document 3: require local educational agencies to use funds for direct or indirect instructional services
limits to 10% the portion of such funds that may be used for indirect instructional services

topic.name: U.S. Policy on Iraq
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of U.S. legislative measures and contingency planning related to military, humanitarian, and economic support in Iraq.

1: contingency planning for a redeployment of Armed Forces from Iraq
report to the congressional defense and appropriations committees on the status of planning for the redeployment
2: withholds specified percentages of Economic Support Fund assistance for Iraq
Secretary of State certifies to the appropriate congressional committees that U.S. government representatives have presented an oil trust plan
3: expresses the sense of Congress concerning Iraqi refugees and internally displaced persons
requires that related fund monitoring mechanisms be established

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at improving educational outcomes and services for specific populations, including Native Hawaiians and at-risk students.

Document 3: Amends the School Dropout Prevention program under part H of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.
Requires state grantees to create and periodically update a research-based Statewide Graduation Success Plan addressing the factors associated with the risk of not graduating from high school.

topic.name: U.S. Infrastructure and Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing the United States' infrastructure and security systems to address various national challenges.

Document 1: Commission on Catastrophic Disaster Risk and Insurance to assess the condition of the property and casualty insurance and reinsurance markets
recommend and report legislative and regulatory changes
Document 2: comprehensive strategy for gaining operational control of the international borders
requires an analysis of staffing requirements and infrastructure needs
Document 3: establishes the United States Commission on Rebuilding America for the 21st Century
contains specific recommendations on policies and investments to provide for streamlined state and local transportation investment processes

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to amendments and provisions in federal health care programs, including Medicare and Medicaid.

Document 1: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Department of Health and Human Services to research and report on medication adherence with respect to federal health care programs.
the bill amends titles XI (General Provisions) and XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide Medicare coverage for screening computed tomography colonography (CTC) as a colorectal cancer (CRC) screening test.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to submit a preliminary report to Congress on the status of coverage of CTC as a CRC screening test under Medicare
Document 3: This bill amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to modify requirements regarding a state plan for medical assistance.
the bill specifies that a state may determine whether a provider is qualified to perform such services.

topic.name: Veterans and Family Support Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving support and benefits for veterans and their families, addressing issues such as disability claims, domestic violence leave, and child protection.

Document 1: Veterans Disability Benefits Claims Modernization Act of 2008
requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to conduct a study on adjusting the schedule for rating veterans' disabilities
Document 2: Domestic Violence Leave Act
entitles an eligible employee, his or her same-sex spouse, or domestic partner to up to 12 workweeks of leave during any 12-month period
Document 3: Child Protection Improvements Act of 2008
directs the Attorney General to establish a program for national criminal history background checks for child-serving organizations

topic.name: FAA Funding and Programs
topic.description: Legislative acts related to the funding and operational programs of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA).

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) out of the Airport and Airway Trust Fund for FY2012-FY2015
Requires the FAA Administrator to include in the Airway Capital Investment Plan a list of capital projects that are part of the Next Generation Air Transportation System (NextGen).
Document 2: reauthorizes through FY2017 the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) and specified FAA programs
extends through FY2017 the airport improvement program; air navigation facilities and equipment; research, engineering, and development; and essential air service.

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conservation, and utilization of public lands for various purposes, including military, recreational, and environmental interests.

Document 1: Allows the Secretary concerned to authorize the use of withdrawn land for additional defense-related purposes
Requires the Secretary concerned to maintain a program of decontamination of contamination caused by defense-related uses on the withdrawn lands.
Document 2: Establishes the Tule Springs Fossil Beds National Monument consisting of approximately 22,650 acres of public land
Requires the Secretary to develop a management plan that provides for the long-term protection and management of the Monument.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to issue a special use authorization to each of the 20 owners of a water storage, transport, or diversion facility
Authorizes the Secretary, in a special use authorization under this Act, to allow the use of motorized equipment and mechanized transport for operation, maintenance, or reconstruction of a facility.

topic.name: Immigration Law and Enforcement
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations aimed at improving the integrity, security, and administration of immigration processes in the United States.

Document 1: Protecting Immigrants From Legal Exploitation Act of 2013 - Amends the federal criminal code to subject a person to fine, imprisonment, or both, who knowingly or recklessly executes a scheme in connection with any federal immigration law-related matter to defraud a person.
Authorizes the Attorney General to bring a civil injunction against an immigration service provider engaging in fraudulent conduct.
Document 2: This bill directs the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to identify aliens, when they apply for an immigration benefit or are placed in an immigration enforcement proceeding, who are serving, or have served, as a member of a regular or reserve component of the Armed Forces on active duty.
annotate all DHS immigration and naturalization records relating to an identified alien to reflect such identification.
Document 3: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to require that visa and admissions petitions and applications filed with the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) or a consular officer must contain all required signatures.
No petition or application may be approved unless a background check is completed to determine whether each petitioner/applicant or beneficiary/derivative is a national security threat.

topic.name: Housing and Veteran Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving housing conditions and support services for vulnerable populations, including veterans and low-income families.

Document 1: Amends the Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act of 1992 to revise the purpose for grants for lead-based paint hazard reduction in target housing.
Requires such grants to be made instead for reduction of lead-based paint hazards and correction of other housing-related hazards.
Document 2: Increases the per diem payment for transitional housing assistance for homeless veterans who are placed in housing that will become permanent upon the termination of such assistance.
Extends supportive services assistance for very low-income veteran families in permanent housing.
Document 3: Amends the Food Security Act of 1985 to extend assistance to disadvantaged or beginning farmers or ranchers for access to the environmental quality incentives.
Provides assistance for agricultural rehabilitation and vocational training for veterans.

topic.name: Cancer Research and Care Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving cancer detection, treatment, and patient-centered care through research, education, and comprehensive health programs.

Document 1: Lung Cancer Mortality Reduction Act of 2008
comprehensive program to achieve a 50% reduction in the mortality rate of lung cancer
Document 2: Patient Centered Quality Care for Life Act
integrated and patient-centered care for managing their pain and symptoms
Document 3: Prostate Cancer Detection Research and Education Act
develop and validate a test or tests to detect and diagnose prostate cancer

topic.name: Regulatory Protections in Commerce
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating and protecting specific commercial practices and welfare standards in various industries.

Document 3: Prohibits social media service providers from intentionally or knowingly using for a commercial purpose a self-image uploaded by a minor.
Sets forth the enforcement authority of the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) and state attorneys general.

topic.name: Due Process and Human Rights
topic.description: This cluster addresses issues related to due process rights and human rights protections for individuals in detention, both domestically and internationally.

Document 1: Due process provided under the Constitution
Prohibits any person detained... from being transferred to the custody of the Armed Forces
Document 2: training to protect the rights of U.S. citizens in foreign custody
due process and human rights violations that are being committed against such U.S. citizens
Document 3: Prohibits the issuance of a visa to a citizen... until the President certifies...

topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation related to the management, regulation, and economic impact of oil and gas exploration and production on federal and state lands.

Document 1: Lease Extension and Secure Energy Act of 2011 directs lease term extensions for oil and gas in the Gulf of Mexico.
Applies to leases not producing as of April 30, 2010.
Document 2: Empower States Act of 2013 amends the Mineral Leasing Act regarding oil and gas exploration regulations.
Requires federal consultation with states and tribes for new guidelines on oil and gas.
Document 3: Using Our Energy To Build Better Communities Act amends the Mineral Leasing Act regarding funds from oil and gas leases.
Reduces reclamation fund allocation and mandates fund use for county schools and roads.

topic.name: Tax Incentives for Transportation and Energy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at providing tax incentives related to transportation infrastructure and renewable energy production.

Document 1: reduces the federal excise tax on gasoline
corresponding increases in fuel taxes imposed by states
Document 2: tax credit for railroad track maintenance
expand the types of maintenance expenditures eligible for such credit
Document 3: extend the second generation biofuel producer tax credit
depreciation allowance for second generation biofuel plant property

topic.name: Legislation on Security and Protection
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts focused on the protection and security of national interests, including veterans' memorials, port security, and trade regulations.

Document 1: Protect our Veterans Memorials Act of 2009
extend federal jurisdiction over the destruction of veterans' memorials
Document 2: Port Security Grant Act of 2011
extends the authorization of appropriations for the port security grant program
Document 3: Drawback Simplification Act of 2007
revises eligibility requirements for the drawback of duties on merchandise exported from the United States

topic.name: Telecommunications and Technology Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to telecommunications, electronic equipment, and consumer rights in the context of technology access and usage.

Document 1: Amends the Digital Millennium Copyright Act (DMCA) with respect the prohibition on the circumvention of technological measures
Expands the exceptions to such prohibition to allow the user of a computer program to connect to a different wireless telecommunications network
Document 2: Directs each federal agency to identify useful federal electronic equipment that the agency has determined is excess to its needs
Defines 'federal electronic equipment' to include computers and peripheral equipment, fax machines, and software
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to extend the exemption from retransmission consent requirements
Limits the definition of 'local market' in the case of both commercial and noncommercial television broadcast stations

topic.name: Regulatory Reform and Accountability
topic.description: This topic covers legislative measures aimed at reviewing, amending, or repealing federal regulations to reduce burdens on businesses and improve the efficiency of regulatory processes.

Document 1: Establishes the Retrospective Regulatory Review Commission to review the Code of Federal Regulations
requires agencies to repeal a rule or set of rules as recommended by the Commission
Document 2: Authorizes each member state under the Streamlined Sales and Use Tax Agreement
minimum simplification requirements relating to the administration and collection of sales and use taxes
Document 3: Defines 'major rule' and 'major guidance' for purposes of this Act
Revises procedures for rule making under the Administrative Procedure Act

topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the revision and reauthorization of federal higher education programs and definitions.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 (HEA) to revise and reauthorize HEA programs.

Revises the general definition of an institution of higher education (IHE) to include schools that: (1) admit students whose secondary education was conducted in a home school setting; (2) award degrees...

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning the temporary suspension of duties on specific chemical compounds and materials.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on preformed iodide pellets or powder
composed of iodides of dysprosium, thallium sodium, holmium, thulium and calcium.
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on magnesium zinc aluminum hydroxide carbonate hydrate.
Document 3: extend the temporary suspension of duty on Magnesium aluminum hydroxide carbonate hydrate.

topic.name: Prescription Drug Regulation
topic.description: The documents discuss legislative measures aimed at regulating the approval, competition, and market practices surrounding prescription drugs and generics.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to prohibit a holder of a new, approved drug application from commencing to manufacture, market, sell, or distribute a generic version of such drug
until the expiration or forfeiture of the exclusivity period granted to the generic manufacturer.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to allow the Secretary of Health and Human Services to deny a new drug application if the application fails to include required information on clinical investigations.
Requires the Secretary to develop guidance for the staff of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to ensure that new drug applications are adequately reviewed.
Document 3: Prohibits, as an unfair and deceptive act or practice... any person from being a party to any agreement resolving or settling a patent infringement claim
Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem an applicant to have forfeited market exclusivity if the applicant enters into an agreement that violates this Act.

topic.name: Defense Programs and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing support and fairness in defense-related programs for military personnel and their families.

Document 1: Defense Level Playing Field Act
consider any unfair advantage that an offeror may possess
Document 2: MyCAA Act
tuition assistance for education and training courses
Document 3: WE CARE Act
review of women-specific health services for female members of the Armed Forces

topic.name: Mine and Workplace Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving safety measures and emergency response protocols in mining and other hazardous work environments.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require emergency response plans
Requires installation of portable rescue chambers or refuge
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Labor to promulgate an interim final standard regulating occupational exposure to combustible dust hazards
Participation of employees and their representatives in hazard assessment

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to financial assistance, regulation of loans, and the management of government ownership in financial assets.

Document 1: Amends the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act to expand eligibility for Farm Service Agency operating loans
Deems specified operating-only entities eligible for farm ownership loans.
Document 2: Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 to revise procedures governing the furnishing of closing documents to borrowers
Requires the lender to furnish to the settlement agent, at least four business days before the scheduled date of a real estate settlement, the completed promissory note.
Document 3: Prohibits the federal government from acquiring, directly or indirectly, any ownership interest in a troubled asset
Requires the Secretary to divest the government of any such interest not later than July 1, 2010.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Development on Public Lands
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at promoting the development and management of renewable energy resources on public lands in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to require amounts received from leases under the Geothermal Steam Act to be available for implementing renewable energy development.
Requires the Secretary to complete and finalize the Programmatic Environmental Impact Statement for Solar Energy Development.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of the Senate that the United States should increase substantially the capacity and generation of clean, renewable hydropower.
Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a grants program for increased hydropower production.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to encourage the use of U.S. workers and equipment in mineral resource development.
Amends the Mineral Leasing Act to streamline the permitting process for energy development.

topic.name: Home and Community-Based Services
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving access and funding for home and community-based services for elderly and disabled individuals.

Document 1: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to revise the income eligibility level for home and community-based (HCBS) services for elderly and disabled individuals.
Increases the federal medical assistance percentage (FMAP) for the provision of HCBS waiver services under Medicaid.
Document 2: Amends the National Housing Act to authorize the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to increase the maximum annual premium payments for mortgage insurance.
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act to increase temporarily the maximum Small Business Administration (SBA) participation in a loan on a deferred basis.

topic.name: Paralysis and Indian Health Care
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving health care services and research related to paralysis and the health care needs of Indian populations.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the National Institutes of Health (NIH) to develop mechanisms to coordinate NIH paralysis research and rehabilitation activities
Authorizes such consortia to conduct basic, translational, and clinical paralysis research
Document 2: Amends the Indian Health Care Improvement Act to authorize appropriations to carry out the Act
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to maintain model diabetes projects in existence on October 29, 1992
Document 3: Declares a new national Indian health policy in order to raise the health status of Indians
Authorizes approval of up to a two-year extension of a health profession preparatory pregraduate education scholarship

topic.name: Government Authorization Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts that authorize funding and projects for government-related initiatives.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to carry out a major medical facility project
Authorizes appropriations.
Document 2: Zika Authorization Plan Act of 2016
reauthorize grants for state mosquito control programs
Document 3: Amends the Soda Ash Royalty Reduction Act of 2006
extend the reduced (2%) royalty rate for soda ash.

topic.name: Veterans Educational Assistance Programs
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing educational opportunities and financial assistance for veterans pursuing degrees in various fields.

Document 1: GI Bill STEM Extension Act of 2015
additional Post-9/11 Educational Assistance to a veteran
Document 2: Military and Veterans Educational Reform Act of 2012
conditions under which the Secretary or a SAA may approve a course
Document 3: scholarship program of financial assistance for individuals
enter into an agreement to serve as a full-time Department of Veterans Affairs employee

topic.name: Oil Spill Regulation and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulatory frameworks aimed at preventing and managing oil spills and their environmental impacts.

Document 1: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to repeal the limitation on the liability of the responsible party for damages and removal costs.
Increases the minimum amount of financial responsibility required for offshore facilities to $1.5 billion.
Document 2: Requires Department of Homeland Security to establish and implement enforceable uniform national standards for the regulation of discharges incidental to the normal operation of a vessel.
Issue a rule to revise the standard so that a ballast water discharge will contain less than specified concentrations of living organisms.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to require an exploration plan to incorporate a third-party reviewed response plan to contain and terminate an ongoing discharge of oil.
Requires the Secretary of Energy to study means of improving prevention methodologies and technological responses to oil spills.

topic.name: Federal Recognition of Indian Tribes
topic.description: The documents discuss the federal recognition of Indian tribes in Virginia and related legislative measures for their eligibility for federal services and benefits.

Document 1: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe.
Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits provided to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 2: Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe.
Tribe and tribal members eligible for all federal services and benefits provided to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Document 3: Reauthorizes appropriations for Native American Housing Assistance for Indian tribe affordable housing activities.

topic.name: Violence and Immigration Enforcement
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at addressing violence against women and immigration enforcement within the United States.

Document 1: Strengthen and Fortify Enforcement Act or the SAFE Act - Title I: Immigration Law Enforcement by States and Localities
states or their political subdivisions to enact and enforce immigration criminal penalties
Document 2: Violence Against Women Reauthorization Act of 2012 - Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994
Enhancing Judicial and Law Enforcement Tools to Combat Violence Against Women
Document 3: Stand Against Violence and Empower Native Women Act or the SAVE Native Women Act
includes sex trafficking as a target of the grants to Indian tribal governments to combat violent crime against Indian women

topic.name: Higher Education Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving safety and addressing misconduct in higher education institutions.

Document 1: establish requirements with respect to sexual violence allegations
must provide support services to affected students
Document 2: requires disciplining of any school employee who fails to report sexual misconduct
establishes policies for investigating and reporting such allegations
Document 3: require each institution of higher education to include in its annual security report a statement of policy regarding harassment
defines 'harassment' to include certain conduct undertaken through technological means

topic.name: Veteran and Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving healthcare access and financial assistance for veterans and healthcare professionals.

Document 1: Helping Veterans Save for Health Care Act of 2015
veteran receiving hospital care or medical services for a service-connected disability
Document 2: Physician Payments Sunshine Act of 2007
require quarterly transparency reports to the Secretary of Health and Human Services
Document 3: Autonomy for Psychologists and Social Workers Act of 2007
remove the restriction that a clinical psychologist or clinical social worker provide services in a comprehensive outpatient rehabilitation facility

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, regulation, and environmental impact of energy resources and greenhouse gas emissions in the United States.

Document 1: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry
Defines 'GHG emissions' to mean emissions of a GHG, including stationary combustion source emissions
Document 2: Prohibits oil and gas preleasing or leasing of any area of the Outer Continental Shelf (OCS) that was not available for oil and gas leasing
Requires approval by law of an adjacent state for oil or gas leasing in any area located within 100 miles from the adjacent state's coastline
Document 3: Sets forth circumstances under which the President may issue orders and regulations to stabilize prices for wholesale and retail gasoline
Amends the Energy Policy and Conservation Act to include prerequisites to a drawdown and sale from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve

topic.name: Energy and Financial Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation of energy markets and financial practices affecting energy entities.

Document 1: Public Power Risk Management Act of 2013 - Amends the Commodity Exchange Act
Defines 'utility operations-related swap' as one that is entered into to hedge or mitigate commercial risk.
Document 2: National Oilheat Research Alliance Reauthorization Act of 2011 - Renames the National Oilheat Research Alliance Act
Directs the Alliance to conduct research, development, and demonstration activities relating to oilheat.

topic.name: Rail and Air Safety Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving safety standards for hazardous materials transportation and air quality management.

Document 1: replaces general requirements for design standards of the Federal Railroad Administration (FRA) for pressurized tank cars
establish and begin enforcing a national maximum volatility standard for the transport of crude oil by rail or by barge
Document 2: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from rejecting a state implementation plan (SIP)
Requires the Administrator to continue to implement the final rule commonly known as the 'Clean Air Interstate Rule'
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program
Directs the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to reduce the volume of advanced biofuel and biomass-based diesel required to be blended into transportation fuel

topic.name: Veterans Benefits Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at providing equitable benefits and assistance to members of the Armed Forces and their families.

Document 1: Military Separation Pay Fairness Act of 2014
determines whether former members of the Armed Forces who were separated for a violation of the policy concerning homosexuality... were paid the full amount of separation pay
Document 2: Permits the posthumous transfer of Post 9/11 Educational Assistance
Allows transfers of such entitlements by individuals honorably discharged or released from active duty due to a service-connected disability
Document 3: United States Cadet Nurse Corps Equity Act
deems service of a person as a member of the United States Cadet Nurse Corps between July 1, 1943, and December 15, 1945 to be active military service

topic.name: Mental Health and Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses initiatives and legislation aimed at improving mental health services and training in educational settings, particularly higher education institutions.

Document 1: require the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) to award grants to institutions of higher education to provide and improve mental and behavioral health services
expand outreach and training on college and university campuses
Document 2: assist IHEs in implementing or expanding model undergraduate or graduate curricula on child abuse and neglect
Document 3: develop a policy for federal science agencies to carry out a program of workshops that educate specified federally funded researchers about methods that minimize the effects of gender bias in the evaluation of federal research grants

topic.name: Military Construction and Assistance
topic.description: Legislation related to military construction, funding, and community assistance for military installations.

Document 1: Assisting local communities coping with the impact of base closures and realignments
Authorizes the Secretary concerned to contract for the procurement of municipal services for a U.S. military installation
Document 2: Appropriates funds for FY2013 for the Department of Defense for military construction
Specifies restrictions and authorizations regarding the use of funds appropriated in this title
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to acquire real property and carry out military construction projects
Limits the total cost of construction projects authorized by this title

topic.name: Legislative Reforms and Support Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at reforming existing laws and providing support to specific groups such as Native Americans, reservists, and National Guard members.

Document 1: Repeals outdated provisions regarding treatment of Native Americans
Encouraging Conciliation with Tribes Act
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
Benefit reservists and National Guard members
Document 3: Extends and modifies the temporary reduction of duty
Duty on monocarboxylic fatty acids derived from palm oil

topic.name: Refugee and Immigration Policy
topic.description: Legislative measures and funding related to refugee resettlement and immigration processes in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to work with the heads of other relevant federal agencies to conduct a review of refugee processing
Permits refugees (and their spouses and children) to be admitted to the United States as lawful permanent residents.
Document 2: Authorizes FY2010-FY2011 appropriations for migration and refugee assistance
Authorizes FY2010-FY2011 appropriations for refugee resettlement in Israel.
Document 3: Sets forth border security and enforcement provisions
Makes it unlawful to knowingly hire, recruit, or refer for a fee an unauthorized alien.

topic.name: Campaign Finance Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at amending federal election laws to enhance transparency, limit expenditures, and provide public funding for political campaigns.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to set forth specified expenditure limitations for House of Representatives general elections.
Establishes in the Treasury the Grassroots Good Citizenship Fund, which shall provide public funding for House candidates' expenditures.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) with respect to eligibility and qualifying contribution requirements for Senate election campaigns.
Establishes a Senate Fair Elections Fund.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require the treasurer of each authorized political committee to establish a separate account for contributions.
Prohibits the use of amounts in such account to make disbursements by or on behalf of the committee.

topic.name: Employee Tax Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing tax incentives and deductions related to employee benefits, including health and dependent care expenses.

Document 1: excludes from the gross income of employees the value of any on-premises employer-provided athletic facility
Allows employers a tax deduction for fees, dues, or membership expenses paid to an athletic or fitness facility.
Document 2: establish tax-exempt dependent care savings accounts to pay the employment-related expenses of caring for a dependent
allow a deduction from gross income of up to $5,000 in a taxable year for cash contributions to such accounts.

topic.name: Veterans and Memorials
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the establishment and improvement of memorials and cemeteries honoring veterans and historical figures in the United States.

Document 1: establish a national cemetery in eastern Nebraska to serve the needs of veterans
serve the needs of veterans and their families
Document 2: authorize the establishment of a memorial to honor slaves and free black persons who served during the American Revolution
Document 3: establish a memorial on specified federal land in the District of Columbia

topic.name: Credit Union Regulation and Lending
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative measures and regulations governing credit unions, particularly regarding their lending practices and capital requirements.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to prohibit an insured credit union from making any member business loan that would result in the total amount of such loans outstanding at that credit union at any one time exceeding either: (1) 1.75 times the actual net worth of the credit union, or (2) 12.25% of the total assets of the credit union.

Directs the Board to develop a tiered approval process, including lending standards, under which an insured credit union gradually increases the amount of member business lending in a manner consistent with safe and sound operations.
Document 2: Amends the Bank Holding Company Act of 1956 to prohibit a bank holding company from holding more than 10% of the total amount of deposits of insured depository institutions in the United States.
Prescribes leverage ratio requirements for operating subsidiaries of bank holding companies and financial companies.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to empower federal credit unions to receive payments on certain uninsured non-share accounts.
Redefines 'net worth' with respect to any insured credit union (other than a low-income credit union) to include uninsured non-share capital accounts.

topic.name: Social Security Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Social Security Act affecting benefits, fees, and health insurance provisions.

Document 1: Amends title II of the Social Security Act with respect to fees of persons representing claimants
Increases the maximum level of fees authorized to be charged by such representatives
Document 2: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to authorize the Commissioner of Social Security to make an adjustment of the percentage increase in OASDI benefits
Ensure that such increases are in no case less than the percentage increases in compensation of Members of Congress
Document 3: Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act to permit the disabled surviving spouse of an individual to elect to retain a private group health plan
Under the Medicare program
